description: You are a Water Resource Specialist preparing a flood damage assessment for the White River through Muncie, Indiana. Quantify how long each cross-section in the HEC-RAS model experiences flooding above a defined threshold by analyzing the unsteady simulation results located at /home/ga/Documents/hec_ras_projects/Muncie/. Write and execute a Python script at /home/ga/Documents/hec_ras_results/flood_duration_analysis.py that extracts water surface elevation (WSE) time series from the plan HDF result file. For each cross-section, define the flood threshold as the WSE at the first time step plus 2.0 feet. The script must compute the total duration in hours above the threshold, the peak WSE, and the percentage of total simulation time above the threshold. Save the results to /home/ga/Documents/hec_ras_results/flood_duration.csv with the columns: CrossSection, ThresholdElevation_ft, FloodDuration_hours, PeakWSE_ft, and TimeAboveThreshold_pct. Additionally, print a summary identifying the cross-section with the longest flood duration. Available libraries include h5py, numpy, pandas, matplotlib, and scipy.
